_comment: Master resume data. /tailor-resume copies this to variants/<name>.json, edits, and renders to .docx. Keep this as the complete source of truth; trim in variants, don't fabricate.
name: Adam Breaux
summary: Operator who deeply understands how companies work and gets into the weeds to get things done. Three years across the full venture lifecycle, from 0-to-1 company builds to fund operations to investment sourcing and diligence. Deep business-evaluation and financial-modeling skills, relentless execution, and fluency with the latest AI tooling.

items.title: Associate (Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead)
bullets: Led Venture 5 from a blank page to a funded company: months of discovery and expert interviews, product ideation, team hiring, first customer contracts, and seed fundraising.

Built fund operations from scratch: defined the metrics that mattered at venture and studio level, stood up tracking and stakeholder reporting, and ran LP reporting that told each venture's story (the good and the bad). Became the direct point of contact LPs come to for updates.
Stood up the studio's entire back office (HR, payroll, accounting) from zero.
Conducted financial modeling and competitive analysis across 500+ potential competitors; computed fund performance (TVPI) for use in fundraising.
Developed the Fund II narrative, pitch, target-investor database, and the HubSpot CRM behind investor relationships; ran the same fundraising process for two in-house ventures.
Led the studio's annual LP meeting end-to-end — logistics and invites through the studio financials and projections presented to LPs.
Led automation projects across ventures, building GTM tooling to remove bottlenecks in portfolio companies' processes.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Diligence Support
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation
bullets: As 1 of ~12 fellows, sourced 14 of 20 finalists and 4 of 7 startups selected to pitch in Tulsa — the most of any fellow.
Ran screening calls, diligenced founding teams, assessed commercial viability, and presented investment theses to internal committees.
Recalled by Atento principals to support diligence on two Core Fund I deals: bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, and review of financial projections, delivered directly to a GP.

items.title: Founding President
items.org: starTUp — University of Tulsa Venture Capital Club
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club and connected members as analysts to the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund, a multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund.
Built a portfolio-support team delivering real work to HVF portfolio companies and created the 'Demystifying VC and PE' panel with local partners.

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee
items.org: University of Tulsa Student Investment Fund  —  $8M student-run fund
bullets: Implemented a new ETF strategy and a new meeting / decision-making process, both still in use today.
Made buy/sell decisions on macro trends and led analysts to execute strategy.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
items.org: The University of Tulsa  —  Division I Cross Country & Track and Field

sections.heading: SKILLS & STRENGTHS
items: Business evaluation & judgment (demand, model, market, financials)
Investment sourcing & diligence; market sizing; competitive analysis
0-to-1 venture building; process design; operations from scratch
Extreme fluency with modern AI tooling
Relationship-driven, relentless execution, root-cause problem solving
